symbols: Solomon enthroned in royal garments — divine wisdom given to the king for righteous judgment
soldier holding child by the foot and raising sword — the terrible test to reveal the true mother
kneeling mother pleading — sacrificial love that would rather surrender the child than see it killed
courtiers and officials watching — the public nature of royal justice
columned palace — the grandeur of Solomon's court and the seat of judgment
description: In a columned palace hall, King Solomon sits enthroned at the left in golden royal garments, his hand extended in a gesture of judgment. Before him, a woman in blue kneels, her hands clasped in desperate supplication. To the right, a muscular soldier holds a living infant upside down by one foot while raising a sword, preparing to carry out Solomon's decree. Another woman stands nearby watching. A crowd of courtiers, priests, and officials observes from behind. The architecture features ornate columns and a raised platform for the throne.
theological_description: The Judgment of Solomon is the paradigmatic Old Testament demonstration of divinely-granted wisdom. When Solomon asked God for wisdom rather than wealth or power, the Lord was pleased and gave him 'a wise and discerning heart, so that there has been no one like you before and none like you shall arise after you' (1KGS.3.12). The case of the two mothers claiming one living child became the proof of this gift. Solomon's stratagem — ordering the child cut in two — was designed not to destroy but to reveal: the true mother's love would rather surrender her claim than see the child die. This wisdom from above is 'first pure, then peaceable, gentle, open to reason' (JAS.3.17). Typologically, Solomon's wisdom prefigures Christ, who declared 'something greater than Solomon is here' (MAT.12.42). The true mother's self-sacrificing love anticipates the divine love that would rather surrender its own Son than see humanity perish (JHN.3.16).

scripture_refs.reason: The narrative of Solomon's judgment between the two mothers claiming the living child
Solomon's request for wisdom and God's granting of a discerning heart
Christ declaring something greater than Solomon is here — Solomon's wisdom as a type of Christ
The wisdom from above that is pure and peaceable, demonstrated in Solomon's discernment
